meta.description: Curated demo questions for the guest trial flow. These are chosen to showcase the product well — interesting problems that produce good conversation and demonstrate the AI interviewer's capabilities.

questions.title: Group Anagrams
questions.question: Given an array of strings, group the anagrams together. You can return the answer in any order.
questions.description:
Given an array of strings strs, group the anagrams together. An anagram is a word formed by rearranging the letters of another word. For example, 'eat', 'tea', and 'ate' are anagrams of each other.

Example:
Input: strs = ["eat","tea","tan","ate","nat","bat"]
Output: [["bat"],["nat","tan"],["ate","eat","tea"]]
questions.starter_code:
def group_anagrams(strs: list[str]) -> list[list[str]]:
    # Example:
    # Input: ["eat","tea","tan","ate","nat","bat"]
    # Output: [["bat"],["nat","tan"],["ate","eat","tea"]]
    pass
what_interviewer_looks_for: Identifies sorting or character-count as the key insight
Uses a hash map to group strings by canonical form
Discusses time complexity: O(n * k log k) with sorting or O(n * k) with counting
Handles edge cases: empty strings, single characters
follow_ups: Can you solve this without sorting the individual strings?
What's the time and space complexity?
How would this work if strings could be very long (millions of characters)?

evaluation_rubric.strong: Quickly identifies the hash map approach with sorted key or char-count key, implements cleanly, discusses trade-offs between approaches
evaluation_rubric.weak: Tries brute-force pairwise comparison, can't identify the grouping key, struggles with the hash map pattern

questions.title: Design a URL Shortener
questions.question: Design a URL shortening service like bit.ly. Users can submit a long URL and get a short one. When someone visits the short URL, they are redirected to the original.
questions.description:
Design a URL shortening service. The system should:
- Generate short URLs from long URLs
- Redirect short URLs to original URLs
- Handle analytics (click counts)
- Support custom short URLs
- Handle high read traffic (100:1 read to write ratio)
what_interviewer_looks_for: Clarifies requirements before designing (scale, features, constraints)
Discusses URL encoding strategy (hash vs counter-based)
key_discussion_points: Custom short URLs and collision handling
follow_ups: How would you handle a billion URLs?
What happens if two users submit the same long URL?

expected_depth_by_level.E4: Core encode/decode, database choice, basic caching, simple analytics
expected_depth_by_level.E5: Distributed ID generation, multi-tier caching, sharding strategy, monitoring
expected_depth_by_level.E6: Global distribution, consistency trade-offs, capacity planning, cost optimization

evaluation_rubric.strong: Drives the conversation, asks good clarifying questions, makes deliberate trade-offs, discusses scalability proactively
evaluation_rubric.weak: Jumps into implementation without requirements, misses caching, doesn't consider scale

questions.title: Technical Challenge
questions.question: Tell me about a technical project you led or contributed significantly to. What was the challenge, what did you do, and what was the impact?
what_interviewer_looks_for: Clear STAR structure (Situation, Task, Action, Result)
Specific personal contributions vs vague team efforts
follow_ups: What was the hardest technical decision you had to make?
How did you handle disagreements with teammates?
What would you do differently if you could do it again?
How did you measure the success of the project?

evaluation_rubric.strong: Compelling story with clear ownership, specific actions, measurable results, and genuine reflection
evaluation_rubric.weak: Vague story, can't distinguish personal role, no measurable outcome, no reflection
